# UC-11.1.15 · Exchange Information Store Mailbox RPC Average Latency (Perfmon)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Performance, Availability &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch Outlook and mailbox response times, so slow mail clients point to a host or storage issue we can fix early.*

---

## Description

The Information Store RPC average latency counter tracks how quickly the mailbox database role serves RPC requests; sustained breaches of vendor guidance precede Outlook freezes, search failures, and database failovers.

## Value

Isolates back-end mailbox performance issues from client-access front ends, shortening root-cause analysis during brownouts.

## Implementation

Collect perfmon with `_total` or per-database instances as your monitoring standard requires. Thresholds vary by Exchange version—validate against Microsoft guidance for your build. Investigate storage subsystems when latency rises without CPU saturation.

## SPL

```spl
index=perfmon object="MSExchangeIS Mailbox" counter="RPC Average Latency" earliest=-4h
| eval ms=coalesce(Value, value)
| where ms>100
| bin _time span=5m
| stats avg(ms) as avg_mapi_ms max(ms) as peak_mapi_ms by _time, host, instance
| sort -peak_mapi_ms
```

## Visualization

Timechart per host, table (instance × peak), heatmap.

## Known False Positives

Mailbox RPC spikes during VSS backup, MAPI-heavy migrations, or archive batch jobs.
